title: PersistentMap abstraction + im-rs integration
analysis: The task migrates ValueMap internals from std::collections::HashMap to im::HashMap for O(1) structural-sharing clones. ValueCellId already derives Clone+Hash+Eq, Value already derives Clone+Eq — both satisfy im::HashMap's trait bounds. The current ValueMap API uses &mut self for insert(), which im::HashMap also supports via its mutable insert method (it mutates in place but shares structure on clone). The get() return type (Option<&V>) and iter() signature are also compatible, so consumers (reify-expr, reify-constraints, reify-eval, reify-test-support, boundary tests) need zero code changes — only Cargo.toml dependency wiring and the internal ValueMap storage type change. The PersistentMap<K,V> newtype provides a clean abstraction layer, re-exported from reify-types. Since all 122 existing tests exercise ValueMap through its public API, they serve as the regression suite for the migration.

prerequisites.description: Add `im` crate (version 15.x) as a workspace dependency in root Cargo.toml and add it to reify-types/Cargo.toml dependencies

steps.description: Write unit tests for PersistentMap in crates/reify-types/src/persistent.rs: new/default creates empty map, insert+get round-trip, insert returns updated map without mutating original (functional style), contains_key, len/is_empty, remove, keys/values/iter, FromIterator, IntoIterator, Clone is O(1) structural sharing (clone then mutate clone doesn't affect original), Debug/PartialEq/Default trait impls

Implement PersistentMap<K,V> newtype over im::HashMap<K,V> in crates/reify-types/src/persistent.rs with all methods: new(), get(), insert() (mutable &mut self), insert_functional() (returns new map), remove(), contains_key(), len(), is_empty(), iter(), keys(), values(). Derive/implement Clone, Default, Debug, PartialEq. Implement FromIterator and IntoIterator. Add `pub mod persistent;` and re-export `PersistentMap` from lib.rs
Write a test in persistent.rs that verifies PersistentMap works with ValueCellId keys and Value values (the concrete types used by ValueMap), confirming trait bound compatibility before migration

Migrate ValueMap in crates/reify-types/src/value.rs: change `inner: HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>` to `inner: PersistentMap<ValueCellId, Value>`, remove `use std::collections::HashMap`, update import to use PersistentMap. All existing ValueMap methods (new, get, insert, contains, len, is_empty, iter, get_or_undef) remain unchanged in signature

Run `cargo test --workspace` to verify all 122 existing tests pass with the migrated ValueMap. This confirms API compatibility across reify-expr, reify-constraints, reify-eval, reify-test-support, and all boundary tests

Write a test in value.rs verifying ValueMap clone produces structural sharing: clone a ValueMap, insert into the clone, verify original is unmodified and clone has the new entry — confirming O(1) clone semantics propagate through the ValueMap wrapper
Verify no remaining `std::collections::HashMap` usage for ValueMap internals. Clean up any unused imports. Final `cargo test --workspace` confirmation

design_decisions.decision: PersistentMap provides both mutable insert(&mut self) and functional insert_functional(&self) -> Self APIs
design_decisions.rationale: im::HashMap supports both styles. The mutable API preserves backward compatibility with existing ValueMap::insert(&mut self). The functional API is exposed for future M2 snapshot workflows where callers want to create new versions without mutating the original.

design_decisions.decision: ValueMap keeps its existing &mut self insert API unchanged
design_decisions.rationale: All existing consumers (reify-eval Engine::eval, constraint tests, expr tests) use `values.insert(id, val)` with mutable references. Changing to a functional API would require rewriting all consumers for zero benefit at this stage. The structural sharing benefit comes from clone, not from the insert style.

design_decisions.decision: PersistentMap is a newtype (tuple struct) over im::HashMap, not a type alias
design_decisions.rationale: A newtype provides encapsulation — we can change the backing implementation later (e.g., to a custom HAMT) without affecting consumers. It also lets us add domain-specific methods and control which im::HashMap APIs are exposed.

design_decisions.decision: No changes needed to consumer crates (reify-expr, reify-eval, reify-constraints, reify-test-support)
design_decisions.rationale: ValueMap's public API (get, insert, contains, len, is_empty, iter, get_or_undef) is fully compatible with im::HashMap's API. The change is purely internal to reify-types. This was verified by inspecting all consumer usage patterns — they all go through ValueMap's methods, never accessing `.inner` directly.

Latest stable release line. Provides Hash-Array Mapped Trie (HAMT) implementation with O(1) clone via structural sharing, O(log n) get/insert/remove — the exact properties needed for M2 snapshot cloning.

reuse.what: ValueCellId's existing Clone + Hash + Eq derives
reuse.how: ValueCellId already satisfies im::HashMap's key trait bounds (Clone + Hash + Eq) — no modifications needed

reuse.what: Value's existing Clone derive and manual PartialEq/Eq impls
reuse.how: Value already satisfies im::HashMap's value trait bound (Clone) — no modifications needed
All method signatures (get, insert, contains, len, is_empty, iter, get_or_undef) are preserved exactly, only internal storage changes

reuse.what: Existing 122 test suite as regression tests
reuse.how: Running `cargo test --workspace` after migration validates that the im::HashMap drop-in is API-compatible with no consumer changes needed
